scope.summary:
Goal: CAPSTONE end-to-end proof that the whole three-tier diagnostic system works as an integrated system on the real binaries (Master Spec v02.196 §5.8 + §6.13 + §10.12.5; HBR-INT-009). This MT runs the full system — real HandshakeApp + the real spawned palmistry.exe + the real shared-memory ring — through the four scenarios the WP exists to handle, proves each, asserts the typed-allowlist privacy invariant holds across EVERY artifact, and writes a proof manifest. It is integration-level: it WIRES the pieces from MT-081..015 together and proves them as a whole; it does not re-implement them.

THE FOUR END-TO-END SCENARIOS (each a real run, no mocks for the spec-required runtime):
- (1) FORCE A FREEZE: launch the real HandshakeApp (which spawns palmistry.exe, MT-094), then STALL the UI thread (e.g. a test seam / a test-only pane that blocks the egui frame loop past the ~5s threshold). Assert: the heartbeat goes stale (MT-084), Palmistry detects the freeze via stale-heartbeat + hung-window probe (MT-091), Palmistry CAPTURES + SURVIVES (it is still running while Handshake is frozen, MT-089/010), and RECORDS the last state into the survivor store (MT-093). Palmistry read what Handshake wrote even while Handshake was stalled (zero cooperation, MT-090).
- (2) FORCE A CRASH: launch the real app + Palmistry, then force a CRASH (a hard unhandled exception / abort on a test seam — NOT a clean shutdown). Assert: Palmistry writes a MINIDUMP out-of-process (MT-092), SURVIVES the parent death (MT-089), records the crash into the survivor store (MT-093), and the minidump validates with the minidump reader. A CLEAN shutdown in the same harness produces NO crash record (the §6.13 clean-shutdown gate).
- (3) RE-RUN THE BACKEND-DOWN REPRO (MT-088): launch the real app with the backend (127.0.0.1:37501) DOWN; assert the app stays RESPONSIVE (frames complete within a tight bound, the heartbeat keeps advancing, a BackendUnreachable event recorded, a degraded UI shown) — the 2026-06-26 freeze does NOT recur. (This re-proves MT-088 at the integrated level.)
- (4) TYPED-ALLOWLIST PRIVACY PROOF: across ALL artifacts produced by scenarios 1-3 — every ring record, the heartbeat, the survivor store records, the minidump METADATA (the typed crash record, not the dump's OS image bytes), and the FR-forward body — assert NO project content / NO sensitive data / NO free text appears: only typed event codes, ids, counters, phase markers, thread ids, resource metrics, timestamps, and local path references. A scan of the emitted artifacts confirms the typed-allowlist invariant (§5.8.3 + §6.13.8) holds system-wide.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_three_tier_end_to_end.rs` — the capstone integration test driving scenarios 1-4 against the real HandshakeApp + the built palmistry.exe (the test must build/locate palmistry.exe via the external target dir, the same current_exe()-relative resolution MT-094 uses). Gate the live-backend-up parts (scenario 3 recovery, MT-093 FR forward) behind the `integration`/requires_pg feature where a managed backend is needed; the freeze/crash/backend-down-responsive halves run WITHOUT a backend (the freeze + the backend-DOWN case need no live backend by definition).
- CREATE any minimal TEST-ONLY seams needed to force the freeze/crash deterministically (a cfg(test)/feature-gated stall pane + a crash trigger) in the smallest possible footprint — these are test harness seams, NOT production behavior, and must NOT be reachable in the shipped binary (gate behind a test feature). If a tiny production hook is unavoidable to make the freeze/crash injectable, keep it test-feature-gated + document it.
- CREATE the PROOF MANIFEST: emit a `three_tier_end_to_end_proof_manifest.json` to the external artifact root listing each scenario, its verdict (PASS/typed-blocker), the artifacts produced (survivor record paths, minidump path, screenshots), and a `ThreeTierDiagnosticWiringRecord` (MT-095 format) for the whole WP showing all three tiers WIRED with proof_refs. Also emit a wgpu screenshot of the Diagnostics Panel (MT-087) showing live heartbeat/frame/resource + (post-recovery) a forwarded freeze/crash record — visually inspected (GLOBAL-INSPECT).
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_three_tier_privacy_allowlist.rs` — the system-wide typed-allowlist scan (scenario 4) over the emitted artifacts.

HONESTY (Spec-Realism + the WP's no-mock rule): scenarios 1-3 must use the REAL app + the REAL palmistry.exe + the REAL ring — not mocked tiers. Where a managed backend is genuinely unavailable in the env (the FR-forward live round-trip, MT-093), gate that half as requires_pg/integration and record it as NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF — do NOT fake it. A partial-but-honest capstone (freeze + crash + backend-down all PASS on real binaries; the FR-forward live half gated) is the expected acceptable outcome if no managed backend is present.

Reuse note: every tier is built in MT-081..014; the evidence format is MT-095. This MT WIRES + PROVES them together — it reuses the real APIs, the real spawn, the real ring. The screenshot + external-artifact discipline is the established WP-012 pattern (external Handshake_Artifacts root; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).

Risk if missed: the three tiers are each proven in isolation but never as an integrated system, so a wiring gap between them (e.g. Handshake writes a ring Palmistry maps differently, or a freeze the detector misses end-to-end) goes uncaught; the 2026-06-26 repro is not re-proven at the integrated level; the privacy invariant is asserted per-MT but never system-wide.
acceptance_criteria: AC-016-1 (FREEZE end-to-end): the real HandshakeApp (with the real spawned palmistry.exe) is forced into a UI-thread freeze; the test asserts Palmistry detects the freeze (stale heartbeat + hung-window probe, MT-091), STAYS RUNNING while Handshake is frozen (MT-089/010 zero-cooperation), and records the last state into the survivor store (MT-093) — captured + survived + recorded, end-to-end on real binaries.

AC-016-2 (CRASH end-to-end): the real app is forced to crash (hard unhandled exception/abort, not a clean shutdown); the test asserts Palmistry writes an out-of-process minidump (MT-092, validated by the minidump reader), SURVIVES the parent death (MT-089), and records the crash into the survivor store (MT-093). A CLEAN shutdown in the same harness produces NO crash record (the §6.13 gate).

AC-016-3 (BACKEND-DOWN re-prove, MT-088 at integration level): the real app launched with the backend DOWN stays RESPONSIVE — frames complete within a tight bound, the heartbeat keeps advancing, a BackendUnreachable event is recorded, a degraded UI is shown — the 2026-06-26 freeze does NOT recur.

AC-016-4 (TYPED-ALLOWLIST system-wide): a scan over ALL artifacts from scenarios 1-3 (ring records, heartbeat, survivor records, minidump METADATA/typed crash record, FR-forward body) asserts NO project content / sensitive data / free text — only typed codes/ids/counters/phase-markers/thread-ids/resource-metrics/timestamps/local-path-references (§5.8.3 + §6.13.8 invariant holds system-wide).

AC-016-5 (PROOF MANIFEST): a `three_tier_end_to_end_proof_manifest.json` is emitted to the external artifact root listing each scenario verdict + artifacts + a whole-WP ThreeTierDiagnosticWiringRecord (MT-095 format, all three tiers WIRED with proof_refs); a wgpu screenshot of the Diagnostics Panel (live heartbeat/frame/resource + a forwarded freeze/crash record post-recovery) is saved external + visually inspected.

AC-016-6 (HONEST GATING): the FR-forward LIVE round-trip (MT-093) that needs a managed backend is gated requires_pg/integration and recorded NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if no managed backend is present — NOT faked. The freeze/crash/backend-down halves run on real binaries WITHOUT a backend and PASS.

AC-016-7: any freeze/crash injection seam is TEST-FEATURE-GATED + not reachable in the shipped binary (source review). `cargo build -p handshake-native` + `-p palmistry` clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external target dirs; `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty; no repo-local target/.
proof_targets: PT-016-A: `cargo test -p handshake-native three_tier_end_to_end` GREEN — freeze (capture+survive+record) on real binaries.
PT-016-B: crash end-to-end GREEN — out-of-process minidump validated + survive + record; clean-shutdown-no-crash gate.
PT-016-C: backend-down re-prove GREEN — responsive + heartbeat advances (2026-06-26 repro does not recur).

PT-016-D: `cargo test three_tier_privacy_allowlist` GREEN — system-wide typed-allowlist scan; proof manifest + Diagnostics Panel screenshot emitted external + inspected; FR-forward live half gated NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if no backend; both crates build clean + clippy -D warnings + full suite -j 2 green.
implementation_notes: BOUNDED-TEST RULE (MT-092 precedent, MANDATORY): any test spawning the palmistry binary / doing minidumper-IPC / waiting on a child MUST use an explicit timeout (wait_for_exit/connect/read) - NEVER an unbounded Command::output() or socket read_line. #[ignore] with a typed reason any test that deadlocks under this headless harness (run with --ignored on a real host); minidumper out-of-process IPC + simulate_exception are the known hangers. Run palmistry tests under a hard external bound so a deadlock cannot waste hours.
This is INTEGRATION: wire + prove MT-081..015 together on the REAL HandshakeApp + the REAL palmistry.exe + the REAL ring. Do not re-implement any tier; reuse their real APIs/spawn/ring.
Locate the built palmistry.exe via the external target dir / current_exe()-relative resolution (the same MT-094 path). The capstone needs both binaries built; run with the external CARGO_TARGET_DIR.

Freeze/crash injection must be DETERMINISTIC + TEST-FEATURE-GATED (a cfg/feature stall pane + a crash trigger) — NOT reachable in the shipped binary. Keep the footprint minimal; document any unavoidable production hook as test-feature-gated.

Scenario 4 (privacy) scans the EMITTED ARTIFACTS (ring records, survivor JSON, crash record metadata, FR-forward body) for any free-text/content — the ring + records are typed by construction (MT-081 schema), so this proves the construction held end-to-end and that the minidump's typed METADATA record (not the OS-image dump bytes) carries no project data.

HONESTY: the FR-forward LIVE round-trip needs a managed backend; gate it requires_pg/integration + record NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if absent. The freeze + crash + backend-DOWN halves need NO live backend and must PASS on real binaries. A partial-but-honest capstone (those three PASS; FR-forward live gated) is the expected acceptable outcome.

Screenshots + the proof manifest go to the external Handshake_Artifacts root only; git ls-files src/**/*.png must stay empty. Visually inspect the Diagnostics Panel screenshot (GLOBAL-INSPECT) before declaring it shows the live + forwarded state.
Emit a whole-WP ThreeTierDiagnosticWiringRecord (MT-095 format) in the manifest showing all three tiers WIRED with proof_refs to the proving MTs.

Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the capstone is the WP's integration gate — the four scenarios must run on real binaries, the no-mock rule applies, and honest gating (not faking) is required where a managed resource is absent.
binds_backend_api: REUSES the MT-093 FR-forward path (existing runtime_chat_event route) for the recovery-forward scenario, gated requires_pg/integration. NO new endpoint, NO FR edit. Freeze/crash/backend-down scenarios need no backend.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: The capstone exercises the Tier 1 FR-forward path end-to-end (MT-093) in the recovery scenario (gated requires_pg if no managed backend, NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF honestly) and records it in the whole-WP ThreeTierDiagnosticWiringRecord manifest.

The capstone proves Tier 2 internal_diagnostics end-to-end: heartbeat (MT-084), backend-down degradation re-prove (MT-088), panic record (MT-083), and the live Diagnostics Panel (MT-087) screenshot — all on the real app.

The capstone proves Tier 3 Palmistry end-to-end on the real spawned palmistry.exe: freeze capture+survive (MT-089/010/011), crash minidump+survive (MT-092), survivor store + recovery forward (MT-093), launched-with-Handshake (MT-094). The whole three-tier system is proven as an integrated whole here.

risks: RISK-016-1: the tiers are proven in isolation but a wiring gap between them (ring layout mismatch, a freeze the integrated detector misses) goes uncaught. Mitigate: real-binary end-to-end scenarios (AC-016-1/2/3).

RISK-016-2: a mocked tier substitutes for the real runtime (violates the WP no-mock rule). Mitigate: real HandshakeApp + real palmistry.exe + real ring; honest gating where a managed backend is absent (AC-016-6).
RISK-016-3: a freeze/crash injection seam leaks into the shipped binary. Mitigate: test-feature-gated, not shipped-reachable (AC-016-7).
RISK-016-4: project content leaks into some artifact only visible system-wide (not caught per-MT). Mitigate: the system-wide typed-allowlist scan (AC-016-4).
RISK-016-5: a faked FR-forward success when no managed backend is present. Mitigate: requires_pg gate + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF honest record (AC-016-6).
RISK-016-6: a tracked PNG / repo-local target leak. Mitigate: external-only artifacts; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty (AC-016-7).
minimum_controls: MC-016-1: freeze + crash + backend-down all proven on real binaries (AC-016-1/2/3).
MC-016-3: honest gating of the FR-forward live half (AC-016-6).
MC-016-4: proof manifest + Diagnostics Panel screenshot (external, inspected); test seams not shipped; artifact hygiene (AC-016-5/7).

research_provenance.field_stack: End-to-end integration of the Crashpad-style three-tier system on real binaries (real client + real out-of-process watcher + real shared-memory ring), forcing the freeze/crash/backend-down scenarios the WP exists for, with a system-wide privacy scan + a proof manifest in the MT-095 evidence format.

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.minidump: 0.26.1 (validates the minidump in the crash scenario)
crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.all_tier_crates: as pinned in MT-081/009/012 (memmap2 0.9.11, interprocess 2.4.2, minidumper 0.10.1, minidump-writer 0.12.0, crash-context 0.7.0, crash-handler 0.7.0, sysinfo per MT-086)

research_provenance.motivating_incident: The 2026-06-26 freeze is the origin: scenario 3 re-proves it does not recur (MT-088), and scenarios 1-2 prove the external watcher would have captured it had it happened — the whole three-tier system validated end-to-end against the real incident class.
research_provenance.validation_plan: real-binary freeze + crash + backend-down + system-wide privacy scan + proof manifest + Diagnostics Panel screenshot; honest requires_pg gating of the FR-forward live half; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.
